stem: A electronics testing study measures the battery life (hours) for a sample of 15 smartphone models. The sample mean is 172.5 hours with a sample standard deviation of 65.0 hours. However, 3 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 12 observations have a mean of 164.9 hours and standard deviation of 50.3 hours. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 172.5, 95% CI = [136.5, 208.5]. Without outliers: mean = 164.9, 95% CI = [135.9, 193.9]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [135.9, 208.5]. Point estimate: 168.7 hours.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [135.9, 208.5].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
